correct_answer: Ancient Polynesian cultures used tattoos to signify social status, rank, and achievements.
Maori culture used tattoos known as 'moko' to denote lineage, tribal affiliations, and life story.
Tattoos in contemporary Western societies represent personal identity and self-expression.
